root_text.an3.39:1.2: Mama sudaṁ, bhikkhave, pitu nivesane pokkharaṇiyo kāritā honti.
root_text.an3.39:1.3: Ekattha sudaṁ, bhikkhave, uppalaṁ vappati, ekattha padumaṁ, ekattha puṇḍarīkaṁ, yāvadeva mamatthāya.
root_text.an3.39:1.4: Na kho panassāhaṁ, bhikkhave, akāsikaṁ candanaṁ dhāremi. Kāsikaṁ, bhikkhave, su me taṁ veṭhanaṁ hoti, kāsikā kañcukā, kāsikaṁ nivāsanaṁ, kāsiko uttarāsaṅgo.
root_text.an3.39:1.5: Rattindivaṁ kho pana me su taṁ, bhikkhave, setacchattaṁ dhārīyati:
root_text.an3.39:1.6: ‘mā naṁ phusi sītaṁ vā uṇhaṁ vā tiṇaṁ vā rajo vā ussāvo vā’ti.
root_text.an3.39:2.1: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, tayo pāsādā ahesuṁ—eko hemantiko, eko gimhiko, eko vassiko.
root_text.an3.39:2.2: So kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, vassike pāsāde vassike cattāro māse nippurisehi tūriyehi paricārayamāno na heṭṭhāpāsādaṁ orohāmi.
root_text.an3.39:2.3: Yathā kho pana, bhikkhave, aññesaṁ nivesane dāsakammakaraporisassa kaṇājakaṁ bhojanaṁ dīyati bilaṅgadutiyaṁ, evamevassu me, bhikkhave, pitu nivesane dāsakammakaraporisassa sālimaṁsodano dīyati.
root_text.an3.39:3.1: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, evarūpāya iddhiyā samannāgatassa evarūpena ca sukhumālena etadahosi:
root_text.an3.39:3.2: ‘assutavā kho puthujjano attanā jarādhammo samāno jaraṁ anatīto paraṁ jiṇṇaṁ disvā aṭṭīyati harāyati jigucchati attānaṁyeva atisitvā, ahampi khomhi jarādhammo jaraṁ anatīto.
root_text.an3.39:3.3: Ahañceva kho pana jarādhammo samāno jaraṁ anatīto paraṁ jiṇṇaṁ disvā aṭṭīyeyyaṁ harāyeyyaṁ jiguccheyyaṁ na metaṁ assa patirūpan’ti.
root_text.an3.39:3.4: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, iti paṭisañcikkhato yo yobbane yobbanamado so sabbaso pahīyi.
root_text.an3.39:4.1: Assutavā kho puthujjano attanā byādhidhammo samāno byādhiṁ anatīto paraṁ byādhitaṁ disvā aṭṭīyati harāyati jigucchati attānaṁyeva atisitvā:
root_text.an3.39:4.2: ‘ahampi khomhi byādhidhammo byādhiṁ anatīto, ahañceva kho pana byādhidhammo samāno byādhiṁ anatīto paraṁ byādhikaṁ disvā aṭṭīyeyyaṁ harāyeyyaṁ jiguccheyyaṁ, na metaṁ assa patirūpan’ti.
root_text.an3.39:4.3: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, iti paṭisañcikkhato yo ārogye ārogyamado so sabbaso pahīyi.
root_text.an3.39:5.1: Assutavā kho puthujjano attanā maraṇadhammo samāno maraṇaṁ anatīto paraṁ mataṁ disvā aṭṭīyati harāyati jigucchati attānaṁyeva atisitvā:
root_text.an3.39:5.2: ‘ahampi khomhi maraṇadhammo, maraṇaṁ anatīto, ahaṁ ceva kho pana maraṇadhammo samāno maraṇaṁ anatīto paraṁ mataṁ disvā aṭṭīyeyyaṁ harāyeyyaṁ jiguccheyyaṁ, na metaṁ assa patirūpan’ti.
root_text.an3.39:5.3: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, iti paṭisañcikkhato yo jīvite jīvitamado so sabbaso pahīyīti.
root_text.an3.39:6.4: Yobbanamadamatto vā, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.39:6.5: So kāyena duccaritaṁ caritvā, vācāya duccaritaṁ caritvā, manasā duccaritaṁ caritvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.39:6.7: jīvitamadamatto vā, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.39:6.8: So kāyena duccaritaṁ caritvā, vācāya duccaritaṁ caritvā, manasā duccaritaṁ caritvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.39:7.1: Yobbanamadamatto vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattati.
root_text.an3.39:7.3: jīvitamadamatto vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattatīti.

translation_text.an3.39:1.1: “My lifestyle was delicate, mendicants, most delicate, extremely delicate.
translation_text.an3.39:1.2: In my father’s home, lotus ponds were made just for me.
translation_text.an3.39:1.3: In some, blue water lilies blossomed, while in others, there were pink or white lotuses, just for my benefit.
translation_text.an3.39:1.4: I only used sandalwood from Kāsi, and my turbans, jackets, sarongs, and upper robes also came from Kāsi.
translation_text.an3.39:1.5: And a white parasol was held over me night and day, with the thought:
translation_text.an3.39:1.6: ‘Don’t let cold, heat, grass, dust, or damp bother him.’
translation_text.an3.39:2.1: I had three stilt longhouses—one for the winter, one for the summer, and one for the rainy season.
translation_text.an3.39:2.2: I stayed in a stilt longhouse without coming downstairs for the four months of the rainy season, where I was entertained by musicians—none of them men.
translation_text.an3.39:2.3: While the bondservants, workers, and staff in other houses are given rough gruel with false black pepper to eat, in my father’s home they are given fine rice with meat.
translation_text.an3.39:3.1: Amid such prosperity and such a delicate lifestyle, I thought:
translation_text.an3.39:3.2: ‘When an unlearned ordinary person—who is liable to grow old, not being exempt from old age—sees someone else who is old, they’re horrified, repelled, and disgusted, overlooking the fact that they themselves are in the same situation.
translation_text.an3.39:3.3: But since I, too, am liable to grow old, it would not be appropriate for me to be horrified, embarrassed, and disgusted, when I see someone else who is old.’
translation_text.an3.39:3.4: Reflecting like this, I entirely gave up the vanity of youth.
translation_text.an3.39:4.1: ‘When an unlearned ordinary person—who is liable to get sick, not being exempt from sickness—sees someone else who is sick, they’re horrified, repelled, and disgusted, overlooking the fact that they themselves are in the same situation.
translation_text.an3.39:4.2: But since I, too, am liable to get sick, it would not be appropriate for me to be horrified, embarrassed, and disgusted, when I see someone else who is sick.’
translation_text.an3.39:4.3: Reflecting like this, I entirely gave up the vanity of health.
translation_text.an3.39:5.1: ‘When an unlearned ordinary person—who is liable to die, not being exempt from death—sees someone else who is dead, they’re horrified, repelled, and disgusted, overlooking the fact that they themselves are in the same situation.
translation_text.an3.39:5.2: But since I, too, am liable to die, it would not be appropriate for me to be horrified, embarrassed, and disgusted, when I see someone else who is dead.’
translation_text.an3.39:5.3: Reflecting like this, I entirely gave up the vanity of life.
translation_text.an3.39:6.3: The vanity of youth, of health, and of life.
translation_text.an3.39:6.4: Intoxicated with the vanity of youth, an unlearned ordinary person does bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.39:6.5: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an3.39:6.6: Intoxicated with the vanity of health …
translation_text.an3.39:6.7: Intoxicated with the vanity of life, an unlearned ordinary person does bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.39:6.8: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an3.39:7.1: Intoxicated with the vanity of youth, health, or life, a mendicant disavows the training and returns to a lesser life.
translation_text.an3.39:8.2: and so are old age and death.
translation_text.an3.39:8.3: Though this is how their nature is,
translation_text.an3.39:9.1: If I were to be disgusted
translation_text.an3.39:9.2: with creatures whose nature is such,
translation_text.an3.39:9.3: it would not be appropriate for me,
translation_text.an3.39:9.4: since my life is just the same.
translation_text.an3.39:10.2: I understood the truth without attachments.
translation_text.an3.39:12.4: I’m committed to the spiritual life.”

variant_text.an3.39:1.3: bhikkhave, uppalaṁ → sudaṁ uppalaṁ (bj, pts1ed) | vappati → pupphati (bj, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.39:1.4: akāsikaṁ candanaṁ dhāremi → kāsikaṁ candanaṁ dhāremi (sya-all, km, mr); akāsikaṁ dhāremi (?)
variant_text.an3.39:1.5: Rattindivaṁ → rattidivaṁ (mr) | pana me su taṁ → panassu me taṁ (bj); pana su me taṁ (sya-all, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.39:2.2: paricārayamāno → paricāriyamāno (bj, sya-all, km, mr); parivāriyamāno (pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.39:11.2: Nekkhamme daṭṭhu khemataṁ → nekkhammaṁ daṭṭhu khemato (si, sya-all, pts1ed)
